extracted.title: TE Pipeline Conditions for 4/10/2026
extracted.description:
For Gas Day April 10, 2026, Texas Eastern (TE) has approved and scheduled nominations at each pipeline segment and meter station up to TE's operational capacity. The following locations have been restricted due to requested nominations exceeding TE's operational capacity:

Restricted Locations include: North of Berne; Meter 75919 Deliveries; Receipts 74200 (Planned Outage); Rec from AGT & Del West of Lambertville; TE/AGT Lease (To Lease Capacity); Nexus/TE Leased Meters (To Lease Capacity); 30" South of Berne; Berne South; Danville (Planned Outage); Tompkinsville; Barton; 24"; Sarahsville South; Five Points South; Lebanon; Lebanon Lateral; Batesville South; Deliveries to Meter 74215 (1); Access Area; New Roads South; White Castle South; Union Church; West Monroe Receipts; Longview; Deliveries to the Castor Line; Speaks; Angleton West; East Golden Pass; Gillis.

TABS Pool Restrictions - Tolerances: TABS Pool - Length: 1% or 1,000 (ETX, STX, WLA, ELA, M1-24", M1-30", M2-24", M2-30", M3). TABS Pool - Short: 1% or 1,000 (ETX, STX, WLA, ELA, M1-24", M1-30", M2-24", M2-30", M3).

No-Notice Restrictions: (none listed).

OFO / Imbalance Notice: OFO ---; Action Alert ---; Customer Specific OFO ---.

Imbalance Warnings: Stay Balanced - Access Area, M1-24, M1-30, M2-24, M2-30 and M3.

FERC Order 698: M2-24, M2-30 and M3.

Systemwide Notes: Shippers in Texas Eastern's Access Area Zones and Market Area Zones are required to coordinate any imbalance payback through TE's account representative and actual payback quantities may be limited depending on system operations and conditions. If customers do not adhere to imbalance requirements stated above, TE will utilize any provision of its tariff to ensure system integrity including the issuance of customer specific or system wide OFOs.

Beginning Gas Day March 18, 2026, TE anticipates restrictions of approximately 0-10% of P1 nominations at Danville. For future gas days TE will continue to monitor system conditions associated with the posted planned outages along the 30" system.

Operational advisory for shippers nominating from 79503: TETLP will upper limit to -0- certain activities if shippers (1) have initial nominations originating from 79503 (WLA TABS Pool) for delivery west of EGP at the Timely Cycle and (2) these shippers are consistently reducing these quantities intraday. TE will be reviewing these activities at the Timely Cycle beginning Gas Day January 30, 2026 until further notice.

(1) Beginning Gas Day December 4, 2025 TETCO will begin restricting delivery nominations at MR 74215 - TEXAS GAS TRANSMISSION LLC - LEBANON, OH to the designed meter capacity of 250,000 Dth/d.

Due to gas quality issues, meter 76609 has been shut in and physical receipts at the meter will be unavailable until further notice. Beginning during the Timely cycle for Gas Day December 6, 2025, and until further notice, TE will restrict receipt nominations at Meter 76609 to -0- Dth/d.

If customers/point operators do not manage hourly takes from the system, it will lead to delivery pressures being lower than desired for this area. As a result, TE may be required to impose further restrictions or courses of action in order to maintain the operational integrity of the system, including but not limited to, implementation of flow control at specific delivery meter locations.

TE may be required to issue hourly OFO's to impose further restrictions in order to maintain the operational integrity of the system.

Please contact your Operations Account Representative should you have any questions.
affectedLocations: Rec from AGT & Del West of Lambertville
